notes: Independently seeded MIF-012 merger verification campaigns at the default budgets: boundedness 100 trials x 500 steps (per-step stochastic stimuli) and liveness 1000 trials x 200 steps (shared nominal stimuli).

The Rust surface runs the trials across the rayon pool (bounded here to the 4 taskset cores); the Python floor runs the identical per-trial work sequentially. Reports are bit-identical across Python, Rust sequential, and Rust parallel (per-trial seeding makes the campaign invariant to execution order), so the comparison is pure execution cost.

Non-isolated local comparison run under taskset affinity and moderate host load (load average ~12-17); the rust<python ranking (about two orders of magnitude at these budgets) is robust, absolute numbers are not a production performance claim.
max_ns is recorded per surface; outliers reflect scheduler preemption, not the kernel.

benchmark_context.isolation_method: taskset affinity to logical cores 8-11; no kernel isolcpus reservation
benchmark_context.heavy_jobs_running: shared workstation under moderate concurrent load (load average ~12-17); the rust<python ranking is robust to this load, absolute numbers are not.
benchmark_context.hardware_summary: x86_64, 12 logical CPUs, 6 cores, 2 threads per core
